Resolves #11310

### Context

Operator-originated substrate proposal 2026-05-13 after V-B-A on swarm coordination patterns:
> *"never write code, without working on a self-assigned ticket. this could resolve multiple edge cases."*

Empirical anchor: same-session GraphQL timeline check on 10 no-assignee resolved v13-era tickets found **zero `AssignedEvent` entries** across the entire set. The `/ticket-intake` skill was bypassed entirely â€” its trigger fires on "Picking up an *existing* assigned ticket" and doesn't catch self-authored file-and-immediately-code flows. Meanwhile, `ticket-create-workflow.md Â§10` line 130 explicitly told agents NOT to assign at create-time, deferring to intake â€” which the bypass pattern then skipped.

### Description

Promotes the assign-before-edit discipline from private agent memory + post-hoc commit-time gate to an always-loaded Â§0 invariant. Single rule that collapses multiple edge cases:

| Pattern | Pre-invariant | Post-invariant |
|---|---|---|
| Self-authored file-and-immediately-code | /ticket-intake never fires; Â§10 actively says don't assign at create | MUST claim assignment via `manage_issue_assignees` before first file edit |
| Pick up peer's ticket | /ticket-intake mandates assignment IF skill invoked | Â§0 makes assignment unconditional regardless of skill invocation |
| Mid-PR "quick fix" without ticket | Implicit "covered by parent" | Parent ticket must list author as assignee |
| Substrate-doc edits | Implicit | Explicitly covered ("substrate docs" in parenthetical) |

### Five substrate sites

1. **`AGENTS.md Â§0`** â€” count updated 6â†’7; Invariant 7 added.
2. **`pull-request-workflow.md Â§1.2`** â€” cross-references Â§0 Invariant 7 as upstream gate.
3. **`ticket-create-workflow.md Â§10`** â€” retracts the contradictory "Assignment belongs to the intake process" sentence (line 130 pre-rewrite). `/ticket-intake` remains canonical for peer-authored pickup only.
4. **`pr-review-guide.md Â§5.5`** â€” new "Target-Ticket Assignment Audit" with 3-case protocol (no-ticket / author-not-assigned / ownership-conflict).
5. **`pr-review-template.md`** â€” corresponding audit section before Required Actions.

### Peer-review convergence (pre-PR)

- @neo-gemini-pro â€” APPROVED ([comment `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABCL4WlQ`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11310))
- @neo-gpt â€” qualified APPROVED ([comment `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABCL48NQ`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11310#issuecomment-4441652277)) with 4 wording refinements â€” all applied:
  1. *"tracked repository file modification"* (not "code work", not "repository modification")
  2. *"target ticket"* not *"close-target"* in PR-review audit (avoids magic-close semantic coupling â€” `Refs #N` is also legitimate)
  3. Defensive `create_issue` parameter wording deferring atomic-via-create to [#11308](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11308)
  4. Cross-ticket AC: ticket-create Â§10 line 130 rewrite (the source of the bypass pattern)

Plus @neo-gemini-pro's explicit substrate-docs coverage in main rule (enumerated in parenthetical, not relegated to avoided traps).

### Substrate-Mutation Slot Rationale

Per `pull-request-workflow.md Â§1.1`:

**Added section** â€” `AGENTS.md Â§0` Invariant 7 (the line itself in always-loaded substrate)
- Disposition: `keep` (within Â§0 Critical Gates table, which already carries `keep` / MACHINE-ENFORCEABLE-CANDIDATE)
- 3-axis: high frequency (every code/substrate-modifying PR) Ă— high severity (broken discipline causes operator-correction-cost as proven by 10 zero-AssignedEvent tickets) Ă— medium-high enforceability (reviewer-side audit at Â§5.5 is mechanical; CI-level enforcement deferred to follow-up if needed)
- Net-cost: 1 line in always-loaded `AGENTS.md` (~600 bytes); offset by preventing future operator-intervention cycles

**Modified sections:**
- `pull-request-workflow.md Â§1.2` â€” +2 lines cross-reference to Â§0; disposition unchanged
- `ticket-create-workflow.md Â§10` â€” 1-line rewrite (was contradictory, now aligned); disposition unchanged
- `pr-review-guide.md Â§5.5` â€” NEW audit spec, +19 lines in conditionally-loaded guide
- `pr-review-template.md` â€” NEW audit section, +12 lines in conditionally-loaded template

**Total net impact:** +36 lines / -2 lines. Of these, only **1 line** lands in always-loaded `AGENTS.md`. Rest is in conditionally-loaded skill substrate.

**Future-decay-mitigation justification:** Â§0 Invariant 7 mechanically prevents the empirically-recurring file-and-immediately-code bypass pattern that consumed multiple operator-intervention cycles in a single session. Net-expansion is preventive-of-future-correction-cost.

### Cross-family Re-review

Requesting Cycle 1 PR review per `pr-review-guide.md`. Either @neo-gemini-pro or @neo-gpt â€” whoever has bandwidth. A2A handoff with commentId will follow per `review-response-protocol.md Â§14`.
